name: Luciano Manhães de Andrade Filho
title: Prof. Dr.
bio: Associate Professor IV at UFJF and Team Leader of the UFJF group at CERN/ATLAS. He holds a B.Sc. in Electrical Engineering (UERJ, 2001), an M.Sc. in Scientific Instrumentation (CBPF, 2004, sandwich at CERN), and a Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering (UFRJ/COPPE, 2009, sandwich at CERN), complemented by two post-doctorates — one in PLC communications at UFJF (2009) and another in Nuclear Instrumentation at CERN (2021). He is the Coordinator of the NIPSCERN Lab and a member of the INCT CERN-Brasil (Instituto Nacional de Ciência e Tecnologia para Física de Altas Energias com o CERN). His expertise spans signal processing for calorimetry, FPGA-based systems, and machine learning applied to particle detectors. Web of Science: 1,256 works · 67,244 citations · h-index 118. Scopus: 1,207 works · 78,835 citations · h-index 124. He ranks among the top 0.1% most cited scientists in the world.

awards.title: Most-Liked Video — SEMIC 2025 ("Macro e Microcosmos: Fractais e a Física de Partículas")

name: Chrysthofer Arthur Amaro Afonso
title: B.Sc. Student
bio: Undergraduate researcher at NIPSCERN Lab (UFJF) and CERN collaborator since 2023. FAPEMIG scholar developing extensions to the Lorenzetti framework for 3D calorimeter event visualization and free-running trigger studies (CGV-WEB). Contributes to the SAPHO processor, YANC compilers, POLARIS IDE, and AURORA IDE. Acts as technical co-supervisor (co-orientador) of undergraduate IC students. Member of the INCT CERN-Brasil (Instituto Nacional de Ciência e Tecnologia para Física de Altas Energias com o CERN). Participates in the Nordic Online Logic Seminar (NOLS) and the Young Network for Wittgensteinian Philosophy (YNWP).
awards: Gold Medal — International Physics and Culture Olympiad (IPhCO) (2019, 2021)
Silver Medal — International Physics and Culture Olympiad (IPhCO) (2019, 2021)
Bronze Medal — International Physics and Culture Olympiad (IPhCO) (2019, 2020, 2021)
Gold Medal — Brazilian Astronomy and Astronautics Olympiad (OBA) (2020, 2021)
Gold Medal — Brazilian National Science Olympiad (2021)
Honorable Mention — Brazilian Mathematics Olympiad OBMEP (2018, 2019, 2021)
Most-Liked Video — SEMIC 2025 ("Macro e Microcosmos: Fractais e a Física de Partículas")

name: Gabriel de Aquino Torres
bio: Undergraduate IC scholar (Bolsista CNPq) at NIPSCERN Lab (UFJF), B.Sc. student in Electrical Engineering – Electronic Systems. Contributing to the graphical interface of POLARIS IDE and the YAWT Wave Tracer — SAPHO's signal visualisation tool. Also working on particle detection in calorimetry using deep learning techniques, under supervision of Prof. Dr. Luciano Manhães de Andrade Filho.

name: Ualison Rodrigo Ferreira Dias
title: Me. Dr.
bio: Electrical Engineer (Electronic Systems) focused on Artificial Intelligence, with Master's and Ph.D. from UFJF and a B.Sc. in Mechatronic Engineering from IF Sudeste MG — Juiz de Fora. Former PET-Physics scholar, active in scientific initiation, teaching, extension, research and innovation. Undergraduate work focused on embedded systems and 3D-printing prototyping; master's research applied Fuzzy Logic to indoor environment classification via RF signals. Joined the ATLAS-Brazil collaboration at CERN (Geneva), contributing to FPGA and electronic-system maintenance and upgrades and to new AI-model research. Currently part of a new ATLAS working group applying AI models to electron and photon triggers in the HLT (High-Level Trigger). Also holds an incomplete degree in Physics, complementing a multidisciplinary training.

name: Tiago Aparecido Teixeira
title: Me. Dr.
bio: Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from UFJF and Master in Computer Science from UFV, with a specialization in IT Project Management and a degree in Computer Science. Currently a Systems Analyst at Juiz de Fora City Hall, with academic and professional experience in Data Science, Artificial Intelligence and Computing Systems. As a researcher on the ATLAS experiment (CERN/LHC), worked on online filtering, energy estimation and signal processing under High-Luminosity conditions, implementing deconvolution algorithms and Neural Networks on FPGAs and coarse-grained architectures. Recently expanded to Data Engineering and applied Machine Learning, with bootcamp training and industry work in fintechs and the international real-estate sector, leading predictive-model and Generative AI projects.

name: Pedro Henrique Sereno Romeu dos Santos
bio: He has secondary-secondary education at Escola Estadual Professor Sousa Nilo (2019). He has experience in the area of ​​Electrical Engineering, with an emphasis on Electric, Magnetic and Electronic Circuits.

name: Vinicius Pires Diniz de Oliveira
bio: Electrical Engineering student (Telecommunications) at UFJF. Since 2023, a research fellow on the ATLAS/CERN experiment, focused on energy reconstruction and the development and validation of detection systems. Previously a Telecommunications specialist, with experience in VoIP, PBX, IPv4/IPv6 networks and large-scale technical support. Served as an academic monitor at UFJF, supporting teaching and practical activities. Was Simulations and Research coordinator for the Supernova Rocketry team, leading computational modeling, aerodynamic simulation and structural integration. Also volunteered at the UFJF Science Center Astronomical Observatory, assisting in scientific outreach and instrument operation.

name: Fábio Cardani Luna
title: Me.
bio: Electrical engineer with a Master's in Electrical Engineering and currently a Ph.D. candidate. Experienced in digital signal processing, digital electronics and FPGA development, applied to high-energy-physics instrumentation in the ATLAS experiment (CERN). Research focuses on real-time signal simulation and processing, with emphasis on hardware digital filters — including floating-point IIR architectures to mitigate quantization errors and improve numerical precision. Skilled in large-scale experimental data analysis with Python and C++ (ROOT), covering .root file processing, histogram generation, statistical studies and detection-system performance evaluation. Interested in embedded systems, DSP and large scientific experiments.

name: Leandro da Graça Ribeiro
title: Me.
bio: Electrical Engineer with an emphasis on Electronics, currently working at the European Organization for Nuclear Research (CERN). PhD student in Electrical Engineering at the Federal University of Juiz de Fora, focusing on Electronic Systems. He has a technical course in Electronics from Escola Técnica Pandiá Calógeras (2017). He was an intern at the Federal University of Juiz de Fora, where he also completed his undergraduate and master's degrees in Electrical Engineering, with an emphasis on Electronic Systems.

name: Nelson Cevidanes Nascimento de Assis
title: Me.
bio: Master in Telecommunications Engineering from UFF and currently a PhD candidate in Electrical Engineering from UFJF, working at the intersection between programming, electronic engineering and High Energy Physics. My current research covers Instrumentation, Calorimetry, digital signal processing and FPGA implementation. He has experience in developing computational intelligence algorithms, data analysis in Python/R and modeling optical sensor networks. With a consolidated trajectory in the management of technological projects and entrepreneurship in energy systems (Projeta Energias Renováveis), I apply my expertise to develop analytical instrumentation and control electronics solutions aimed at particle accelerators and highly complex environments.

name: Thiago Campos Acácio Paschoalin
title: Prof. Me. Dr.
bio: EBTT Professor at CEFET-MG, Leopoldina Unit (Department of Electronics), and researcher at CERN on the ATLAS experiment. Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering — Electronic Systems, with emphasis on signal processing, partially developed at CERN. Master's and B.Sc. in Electrical Engineering from UFJF. Specialist in digital signal processing on FPGA devices, especially for Nuclear Instrumentation. Served three full years as a soldier in the Brazilian Navy Corps of Engineers. Experienced with FPGA devices and microcontroller programming. Worked on the PD project for the modem and PLC concentrator for the electrical grid — the 1st Brazilian broadband PLC system for LV networks.

name: Melissa Santos Aguiar
title: Me.
bio: Graduated and Master in Electrical Engineering in the area of ​​Electronic Systems from the Federal University of Juiz de Fora (UFJF). Currently, part of CERN as a Scientific Associate and working as a Technological Development Analyst at the National Synchrotron Light Laboratory (LNLS).

name: Mateus Hufnagel Maranha de Faria
title: Me.
bio: Master in Electrical Engineering — Electronic Systems from UFJF, with a project on digital signal processing and FPGA electronic instrumentation applied to the ATLAS tile hadronic calorimeter (LHC/CERN). Graduated in Control and Automation Engineering from CEFET-MG, Leopoldina. Substitute professor in Automation and Microprocessor Systems at CEFET-MG Leopoldina, working mainly in automation laboratory and PLC activities. Served as Junior Electrical Maintenance Specialist at Smurfit Kappa do Brasil, promoted to Specialist II in 2019, executing electrical and PLC automation projects, maintenance management and retrofit commissioning. Currently a Ph.D. student in Electrical Engineering at UFJF, linked to the High-Energy Experimental Physics and Associated Technologies group (UFJF/CERN) and LAPTEL/UFJF, working on energy reconstruction of the hadronic calorimeter.

name: Kinn Belarmino Batista Dias
bio: Graduated in Electrical Engineering with a degree in Robotics and Industrial Automation from the Federal University of Juiz de Fora. His main area of ​​training is the fields of embedded signal processing in environments with a high rate of events and high energy physics.

name: Dabson Ferreira dos Santos
bio: He has secondary-secondary education at the Colégio Militar de Juiz de Fora (2014). He is currently a PIBIC fellow at the Federal University of Juiz de Fora.

name: Clethie Sediacias Bisseyou Ngatsono
bio: He has secondary-secondary education at Lycée Nganga -Edouard (2017)

name: Arthur Araújo Martins
bio: Graduating in Electrical Engineering - Electronic Systems from the Federal University of Juiz de Fora (UFJF). Currently, he is developing Scientific Initiation research (VIC) in the project ''Functional Extension and Experimental Evaluation of the SAPHO Autoscalable Processor for Embedded FPGA Applications'', with a focus on software documentation. He has a profile focused on academic leadership and technical volunteering, simultaneously serving as Secretary of the Computer Society (CS) and Power Energy Society (PES) Student Chapters of the IEEE UFJF Student Branch. Interested in Embedded Systems, Technical Documentation Management, FPGA Prototyping.
